check_calibration:
Check calibration of confidence estimates.

Calibration uses two signal sources:
1. Auto-collected ground truth from objective outcomes (test pass/fail, command exit codes, lint results)
2. Peer consensus from dialectic agreement (weighted by 0.7 peer_weight)

Objective outcomes are the primary signal. Peer consensus is supplementary.

USE CASES:
- Verify calibration system is working correctly
- Monitor confidence estimate calibration against trajectory/consensus proxy
- Debug calibration issues

RETURNS:
{
  "success": true,
  "calibrated": boolean,
  "accuracy": float (0-1) | null,       // empirical confidence-vs-outcome match from sequential tactical evidence (tests/commands/lint); null when no exogenous outcomes recorded
  "trajectory_health": float (0-1),     // strategic proxy: did confident agents end up healthy? — distinct from accuracy, do not conflate
  "truth_channel": "confidence_outcome_match" | "trajectory_proxy",  // honestly reflects what populated `accuracy` this call
  "confidence_distribution": {
    "mean": float,
    "std": float,
    "min": float,
    "max": float
  },
  "pending_updates": int,              // deprecated (always 0)
  "message": "string"
}

RELATED TOOLS:
- update_calibration_ground_truth: Provide ground truth data for calibration

EXAMPLE REQUEST:
{}

EXAMPLE RESPONSE:
{
  "success": true,
  "calibrated": true,
  "accuracy": 0.87,
  "confidence_distribution": {"mean": 0.82, "std": 0.15, "min": 0.3, "max": 1.0},
  "pending_updates": 0
}

DEPENDENCIES:
- Optional: External ground truth via update_calibration_ground_truth (not required for dynamic calibration)
- Workflow: 1. (Optional) provide external ground truth 2. Call check_calibration to inspect
update_calibration_ground_truth:
Update calibration with ground truth from objective outcomes.

Ground truth comes from observable signals: test results, command exit codes, lint output, build success.
Use this tool when you have a concrete correctness signal to feed back into calibration.

USE CASES:
- Provide ground truth after human review of agent decisions
- Improve calibration accuracy over time
- Enable calibration checking via check_calibration

RETURNS:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Calibration updated",
  "pending_updates": int,  // deprecated (always 0)
  "calibration_status": "string"
}

RELATED TOOLS:
- check_calibration: Verify calibration after providing ground truth

EXAMPLE REQUEST:
{
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "predicted_correct": true,
  "actual_correct": true
}

EXAMPLE RESPONSE:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Calibration updated",

}

DEPENDENCIES:

- Workflow: After human review, call this with ground truth, then check_calibration

rebuild_calibration:
Rebuild calibration from scratch using auto ground truth collection.

Resets calibration state and re-evaluates all historical decisions using the current
evaluation logic (confidence vs outcome quality matching).

WHEN TO USE:
- After updating evaluation logic
- To fix corrupted/biased calibration state
- When calibration shows 100% True or 100% False (no variance)

CALIBRATION LOGIC:
Ground truth now compares confidence to outcome quality:
- High confidence + excellent outcome → True (appropriately confident)
- High confidence + poor outcome → False (overconfident)
- Low confidence + excellent outcome → False (underconfident)
- Low confidence + uncertain outcome → True (appropriately uncertain)

This creates meaningful variance for calibration instead of "was agent healthy?" (always True).

PARAMETERS:
- dry_run: Preview changes without modifying state
- min_age_hours: Minimum decision age to evaluate (default: 0.5)
- max_decisions: Limit decisions to process (default: 0 = all)

RELATED TOOLS:
- check_calibration: Verify calibration after rebuild
- update_calibration_ground_truth: Manual ground truth updates

process_agent_update:
Log work and receive a governance decision (proceed or pause). Auto-creates identity on first call.

WHAT IT DOES:
- Records your update and runs the EISV governance cycle
- Returns a decision (proceed/pause) based on current state
- Provides EISV metrics: Energy (E), Information Integrity (I), Entropy (S), Void (V)

SEE ALSO:
- get_governance_metrics / status() - Check current state WITHOUT logging work (read-only)
- simulate_update - Test governance decision without persisting (dry-run)
- get_system_history - View historical trends over time

ALTERNATIVES:
- Want to check state without logging? → Use get_governance_metrics() (read-only, no update)
- Want to test decision? → Use simulate_update() (dry-run, doesn't persist)
- Want historical data? → Use get_system_history() (time series, not current state)

WHEN TO USE:
- After completing a task or generating output
- After making significant progress or changes

RETURNS:
{
  "success": true,
  "agent_id": "string",
  "status": "healthy" | "moderate" | "critical",
  "health_status": "healthy" | "moderate" | "critical",
  "health_message": "string",
  "decision": {
    "action": "proceed" | "pause",
    "reason": "string explanation",
    "require_human": boolean
  },
  "metrics": {
    "E": float, "I": float, "S": float, "V": float,
    "coherence": float, 
    "risk_score": float,
    "phi": float,
    "verdict": "safe" | "caution" | "high-risk",
    "lambda1": float, "health_status": "healthy" | "moderate" | "critical",
    "health_message": "string"
  },
  "circuit_breaker": {
    "triggered": boolean,
    "reason": "string (if triggered)",
    "next_step": "string (if triggered)"
  },
  "eisv_labels": {"E": "...", "I": "...", "S": "...", "V": "..."},
  "prediction_id": "string | null",            // tactical prediction id; pass to outcome_event to bind (confidence, timestamp) to a recorded outcome. Subject to TTL (default 3600s).
  "warnings": ["string", ...] | null,         // per-call non-fatal warnings, e.g. evidence-record failures from recent_tool_results; inspect to detect silent calibration-loss.
  "recent_tool_results": "(input field) List[ToolResultEvidence] — self-reported tool outcomes iterated server-side under UNITARES_PHASE5_EVIDENCE_WRITE; failures append to warnings."
}

PARAMETERS (most are optional):
- client_session_id (string): Session continuity token (from identity() or onboard())
- response_text (string): Describe what you did (optional but helpful)
- complexity (float 0-1): How complex was your task? (default: 0.5)
- confidence (float 0-1): How confident are you? (optional, auto-derived if omitted)
- task_type (string): "convergent" | "divergent" | "mixed" (default: "mixed")

RELATED TOOLS:
- simulate_update: Test decisions without persisting state
- get_governance_metrics: Get current state without updating
- get_system_history: View historical governance data
- identity: Check/set your identity first

ERROR RECOVERY:
- "agent_id is required": Identity auto-binds on first call - just include client_session_id
- "Authentication required": Call identity() first to set up session binding
- Timeout: Retry with simpler parameters or check system resources

EXAMPLE: Minimal call (identity auto-binds)
{
  "client_session_id": "agent-5e728ecb...",
  "complexity": 0.5
}

EXAMPLE: With work description
{
  "client_session_id": "agent-5e728ecb...",
  "response_text": "Fixed bug in authentication module",
  "complexity": 0.3,
  "confidence": 0.9
}

EXAMPLE RESPONSE:
{
  "success": true,
  "agent_id": "test_agent_001",
  "status": "healthy",
  "health_status": "healthy",
  "health_message": "Coherence 0.85, risk_score 0.23 acceptable",
  "decision": {"action": "proceed", "reason": "Low risk (0.23)", "require_human": false},
  "metrics": {
    "E": 0.67, "I": 0.89, "S": 0.45, "V": -0.03,
    "coherence": 0.85, 
    "risk_score": 0.23,
    "phi": 0.35,
    "verdict": "safe",
    "lambda1": 0.18,
    "health_status": "healthy",

  "eisv_labels": {"E": "Energy", "I": "Information Integrity", "S": "Entropy", "V": "Void Integral"}

- Monitor system health and responsiveness

}

archive_orphan_agents:
Archive orphan/stale agents (manual operator tool — no longer run automatically on a sweep schedule).

TARGETS (auto-classified as archivable):
- Unlabeled agents with 1 update after 3 hours
- Unlabeled UUID agents with 2+ updates after 6 hours
- Ephemeral session-labeled agents after 6 hours

NEVER AUTO-ARCHIVED (visible as ghosts — surface bugs rather than hiding them):
- Agents with 0 updates (still initializing — never checked in)
- Agents with labels/display names
- Agents with "pioneer" / "persistent" / "protected" tags
- Lumen (back-compat label)

outcome_event:
Record an outcome event for EISV validation.

Pairs a measurable outcome (drawing completion, test result, task completion)

- test_failed: A test or validation failed

- task_failed: Agent failed to complete a task

- detail (optional dict): Type-specific metadata (e.g., mark_count, test_name)
- agent_id (optional): Falls back to session-bound agent_id

RETURNS: outcome_id, embedded EISV snapshot at time of outcome

search_knowledge_graph:
DEPRECATED: Prefer knowledge(action="search", query="...") instead — same backend, simpler interface.

Search knowledge graph - returns summaries only (use get_discovery_details for full content).

USE CASES:
- Find discoveries by tags
- Search by agent, type, severity
- Query system knowledge
- Learn from past discoveries
- Full-text search (PostgreSQL FTS or AGE)
- Semantic search (vector embeddings) - find similar meaning, not just keywords

SEE ALSO:
- get_knowledge_graph - Get ALL knowledge for ONE agent (no search)
- get_discovery_details - Get full content for a specific discovery (after search)
- list_knowledge_graph - See statistics (not individual discoveries)
- store_knowledge_graph - Store new discoveries (write, not read)

ALTERNATIVES:
- Want one agent's knowledge? → Use get_knowledge_graph(agent_id="...") (no search needed)
- Want full content? → Use get_discovery_details(discovery_id="...") (after finding via search)
- Want statistics? → Use list_knowledge_graph() (counts, not discoveries)
- Want to store knowledge? → Use store_knowledge_graph() (write, not read)

SEARCH BEHAVIOR:
- Multi-term queries (e.g., "coherence basin") use OR operator by default
  → Finds discoveries matching ANY term (more results)
- If 0 results, automatically retries with individual terms (fallback)
  → More permissive search to help you find relevant content
- Single-term queries: exact match
- Use tags/filters for AND behavior (must match all specified tags)

PERFORMANCE:
- O(indexes) not O(n) - scales logarithmically
- ~0.1ms for typical queries
- Returns summaries only to prevent context overflow

RETURNS:
{
  "success": true,
  "discoveries": [
    {
      "id": "...",
      "summary": "...",
      "has_details": true,
      "details_preview": "First 500 chars...",
      "has_more_details": true,
      "details_length": 1234
    }
  ],
  "count": int,
  "message": "Found N discovery(ies) (use get_discovery_details for full content)"
}

RELATED TOOLS:
- get_discovery_details: Get full content for a specific discovery

- get_knowledge_graph: Get agent's knowledge

- discovery_type: "bug_found" | "insight" | "pattern" | "improvement" | "question" | "answer" | "note" | "exploration"

- status: "open" | "resolved" | "archived" | "disputed"

EXAMPLE REQUEST:
{
  "tags": ["security", "bug"],
  "discovery_type": "bug_found",  # Valid: "bug_found" | "insight" | "pattern" | "improvement" | "question" | "answer" | "note" | "exploration"
  "severity": "high",  # Valid: "low" | "medium" | "high" | "critical"
  "limit": 10
}

FULL-TEXT EXAMPLE:
{
  "query": "coherence",
  "limit": 10
}

SEMANTIC SEARCH EXAMPLE (vector embeddings):
{
  "query": "uncertainty in confidence calculations",
  "semantic": true,
  "min_similarity": 0.3,
  "connectivity_weight": 0.3,
  "exclude_orphans": false,
  "limit": 10
}
Note: Semantic search finds discoveries similar in meaning, not just matching keywords.
      Example: "uncertainty" will find discoveries about "confidence", "certainty", "risk", etc.

onboard:
Register a fresh process-instance with governance. Per identity.md v2 ontology, fresh process-instances mint fresh identity; lineage is declared via parent_agent_id rather than resumed via token. The server gates `force_new=true` automatically when no proof signal is presented.

RECOMMENDED CALL (continuing prior work in this workspace):
 onboard(force_new=true, parent_agent_id="<prior UUID>", spawn_reason="new_session")

RECOMMENDED CALL (genuinely new work, no lineage):
 onboard(force_new=true, spawn_reason="explicit")

WHAT IT DOES:
- Mints a fresh agent_uuid for this process-instance
- Records lineage if parent_agent_id is declared
- Returns a client_session_id for transport-level continuity within this process
- Issues a continuity_token for same-live-process PATH 0 ownership proof; do not use it as a cross-process resume credential

WHEN RESUME IS HONORED:
client_session_id maintains identity within one live process. agent_uuid resume requires a matching continuity_token through identity(agent_uuid=..., continuity_token=..., resume=true). onboard(continuity_token=...) is rejected after S1-c; use force_new + parent_agent_id for cross-process continuity. Without a proof signal, the v2 gate flips `force_new=true` automatically and a [FRESH_INSTANCE] log line is emitted.

⚠️ ANTI-PATTERN — DO NOT auto-inject `continuity_token` between calls at the client transport layer. The token is per-process-instance proof for the PATH 0 anti-hijack gate (Identity Honesty Part C, 2026-04-18), NOT a transport-level identity claim. Auto-injection re-introduces the silent-resurrection vector that Part C closed ("any process knowing UUID X could speak as X"). For cross-process-instance continuity, declare lineage via `parent_agent_id`; do not carry a token forward.

PARAMETERS:
- force_new (boolean): Mint fresh identity. Default posture per v2 ontology; gated automatically when no proof signal present.
- parent_agent_id (string UUID): Predecessor agent. Declares lineage rather than claiming continuity.
- spawn_reason (string): One of "compaction" | "subagent" | "new_session" | "explicit". Audit context for the fork.
- name (string): Cosmetic display label. NOT a resume key — passing name alone does not re-bind to an existing agent (PATH 2.5 retired 2026-04-17). Used as a proof-signal proxy in some legacy flows.
- continuity_token (string): Signed PATH 0 ownership proof. Use with agent_uuid + resume=true for same-live-process proof-owned rebinds; not accepted as token-only onboard/bind resume after S1-c.
- agent_uuid (string): Direct UUID-resume. Requires matching continuity_token under STRICT mode.
- client_session_id (string): Transport-bound session signal. Maintains identity within a process; weak across processes.
- model_type (string): Optional model fingerprint for distinct identity per model.
- client_hint (string): Client type ("chatgpt", "cursor", "claude_desktop", "unknown").
- trajectory_signature (object): Optional EISV signature dict for genesis seeding.
- thread_id (string): Explicit thread to join (auto-derived from session if absent).
- process_fingerprint (object): Declaration-only execution context — never used to resolve identity (issue #123).

RETURNS:
{
 "success": true,
 "uuid": "5e728ecb-...",
 "agent_id": "<display_label>" | null,
 "is_new": true,
 "client_session_id": "agent-5e728ecb...",
 "session_resolution_source": "<how key was derived>",
 "continuity_token": "v1.eyJ...",
 "continuity_token_supported": true,
 "next_step": "Call process_agent_update with response_text describing your work"
}

SEE ALSO:
- identity — check who you currently are (does not mint)
- bind_session — explicit session-rebind for known agent_uuid + client_session_id
- process_agent_update — log work and receive a verdict

REFERENCES:
- (v2 ontology)
- S11 (plugin-side complement) and S13 (server-side complement)
identity:
🪞 Check who this process-instance is, or set a display name.

Per identity.md v2 ontology (Identity Honesty Part C, 2026-04-18): a fresh process-instance is a fresh agent. Lineage to prior work is **declared** via onboard(force_new=true, parent_agent_id=...), not resumed via token. Repeated identity() calls from the same live process return the same identity (within-process via client_session_id); identity() calls from a new process mint a new identity, by design.

⚠️ ANTI-PATTERN — DO NOT auto-inject continuity_token between calls at the client transport layer. continuity_token is per-process-instance proof for the PATH 0 anti-hijack gate, NOT a transport-level identity claim. Auto-injection re-introduces the silent-resurrection vector closed by Identity Honesty Part C ("any process knowing UUID X could speak as X"). If you need identity continuity across a new process-instance, use onboard(force_new=true, parent_agent_id=<prior UUID>, spawn_reason="new_session") to **declare** the lineage; do not resume.

✨ WHAT IT DOES:
- Shows your current identity (UUID + display name)
- Lets you set or change your display name
- Returns client_session_id for transport-level continuity within this process

📝 YOUR IDENTITY HAS TWO PARTS:
- agent_uuid: Your unique ID (server-minted at onboard, never changes during this process-instance)
- agent_id/label: Your display name (you choose, can change anytime; cosmetic per "no lookup by label" doctrine)

SEE ALSO:
- onboard() - Mint identity for a fresh process-instance; declare lineage via parent_agent_id
- get_governance_metrics / status() - Your metrics/state (not identity)
- get_agent_metadata - Detailed metadata (includes identity + more)

PARAMETERS (all optional):
- name (string): Set your display name. Convention: {purpose}_{model}_{date} (e.g., "Riley_m1_20251225"). Cosmetic — does not affect identity resolution.
- client_session_id (string): Within-process session continuity (from previous call this process-instance)
- agent_uuid (string UUID) + continuity_token + resume=true: Explicit PATH 0 resume for the **same live process** that already holds the continuity_token; not for cross-process resurrection (see anti-pattern above).

RETURNS:
{
  "agent_uuid": "5e728ecb-...",  // Your unique ID for this process-instance
  "agent_id": "Riley_m1_20251225",  // Your display name (null if unnamed; cosmetic)
  "client_session_id": "agent-5e728ecb...",  // Within-process continuity; pass on subsequent calls this process
  "continuity_token": "v1.eyJh...",  // Per-instance proof for PATH 0 anti-hijack; do NOT auto-inject between calls
  "session_continuity": {
    "client_session_id": "agent-5e728ecb...",
    "instruction": "Include client_session_id in subsequent tool calls within this process to maintain identity"
  }
}

💡 WHEN TO USE:
- First time in a process-instance: Call onboard() (not identity()) to mint identity for this process
- Naming yourself: identity(name="YourName") to set display name
- Checking status: identity() anytime to see who you are within this process
- Continuing prior work in a NEW process-instance: onboard(force_new=true, parent_agent_id="<prior UUID>") — declare lineage, do not resume

EXAMPLE: Check identity (no parameters)
{}

EXAMPLE: Set your display name
{"name": "Riley_m1_20251225"}

EXAMPLE: Within-process continuity (subsequent calls in same process)
{"client_session_id": "agent-5e728ecb..."}

knowledge:

cleanup_knowledge_graph, get_lifecycle_stats.

ACTIONS:
- store: Store a discovery/insight in the knowledge graph
- search: Semantic search across discoveries (query parameter)
- get: Get all knowledge for a specific agent
- list: Get knowledge graph statistics
- update: Update an existing discovery's status, content/details, summary, tags, severity, or type
- details: Get full details of a specific discovery (discovery_id parameter)
- note: Quick note storage (content parameter)
- cleanup: Run lifecycle cleanup on stale discoveries
- stats: Get lifecycle statistics
